topic: Cursor BugBot Review 2026: AI Security Checks in Every PR

stats.fact: BugBot achieves a 78.13% resolution rate across 50,310 PRs analyzed as of 2026 — 15 percentage points higher than the next-closest AI code review product
BugBot launched out of beta in July 2025 with a 52% resolution rate; it has since climbed to nearly 80% through 40+ major experiments
BugBot processes over 2 million pull requests per month
BugBot has reviewed more than 1 million PRs since launch, flagging 1.5 million potential issues
Over 35% of BugBot Autofix proposed changes are merged directly into the base pull request
More than 110,000 repositories have enabled learned rules, generating over 44,000 custom rules
BugBot flags an average of 0.7 bugs per PR run; resolved bugs per PR more than doubled from ~0.2 to ~0.5 since launch
BugBot Autofix exited beta in February 2026; Fix All multi-finding action launched April 2026
BugBot pricing: Pro and Teams plans both $40/user/month; Enterprise is custom pricing
CodeRabbit (main competitor) starts at $24/dev/month and supports all 4 major code hosts vs BugBot's GitHub + GitLab

angles: Security-first, not style-first: BugBot intentionally skips formatting and low-severity concerns to focus exclusively on logic bugs, security vulnerabilities, race conditions, and edge cases — avoiding alert fatigue

From notification tool to autonomous agent: BugBot Autofix (February 2026) marks a fundamental shift — it does not just flag bugs, it spawns isolated cloud VMs to test and propose fixes autonomously, with 35%+ merge rate

Self-improving AI via learned rules: BugBot trains on real developer reactions (downvotes, replies, missed-bug comments) from 2M+ PRs/month to continuously refine its detection model — 110,000 repos adopted, 44,000+ rules generated

Premium price, premium justification: At $40/seat vs CodeRabbit's $24, BugBot's higher resolution rate (78% vs ~63%) and Autofix integration make the math work for high-volume teams — but solo devs may not need it

Cursor ecosystem lock-in vs. standalone value: BugBot integrates natively with Cursor editor and Background Agent but works on any repo — raising the question of whether non-Cursor teams should pay the premium

Enterprise workflow change: BugBot as a mandatory pre-merge gate changes PR review culture — developers are incentivized to fix AI-flagged issues before requesting human review, compressing review cycles
keywords: AI pull request review security checks
